title: Clock faces / time manipulatives
context: Telling time is a core elementary mathematics topic, and printable clock faces are among the most searched-for free teacher resources. The critical constraint for clock faces is angle accuracy: the 12 hour positions must be at exact 30-degree intervals, and the 60 minute positions at exact 6-degree intervals. A clock face with unequal intervals teaches a false relationship between angle and time. This family is relatively self-contained and a natural addition to the backlog.
objective: Implement a clock-face / time-manipulative generator module with verified angle accuracy for hour and minute positions.
acceptanceCriteria: Generates analog clock faces with 12 hour markers at exactly 30-degree intervals and 60 minute markers at exactly 6-degree intervals; angle accuracy verified by the harness.
Supports blank (no hands), preset-time, and cut-out-hands variants; movable-hand variant provides separate hand pieces to cut and attach with a brass fastener.
Hour and minute numerals are legible at standard and large-print sizes in grayscale.
All standard variants generated (A4/Letter x colour/grayscale x standard/large-print).
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-002.json -- verification harness (extend for angle accuracy check)
output: A clock-face generator module in the engine package, with a spec file and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
